behaviors.description: Exponential backoff delay doubles on each consecutive failure until capped by configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is local backoff math/state progression, not a cross-module failure boundary. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks do not hide this; deterministic inputs expose failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff calculator/state object with a failure sequence (e.g., 1..N) and assert delays are 2^k progression until exactly capped at max.

behaviors.description: Retry logic detects rate-limit responses and derives the correct wait duration for next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior is classification/parsing + wait computation logic for a single retry decision. REAL-INFRA: No real HTTP stack required; crafted response objects are sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: A mock transport does not mask this parser/decision bug. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative response/error shapes (429 status, retry-after headers, non-429) into the retry-decision function and assert whether it retries and what delay it computes.

behaviors.description: After waiting, client resends the original request and returns successful response transparently.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE (quote): "After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result". Adjacent lower-level miss: A Unit test of backoff or request builder can pass while handoff between timer/scheduler and transport drops or mutates the original request; this is a concrete module-interaction failure, not generic wiring. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network required (fake clock + in-process stub transport), so not System; (b) no AI output, so not Agentic; (c) no multi-agent pipeline, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/network conditions, only resend semantics after wait.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry orchestrator + fake clock + stub transport: first call returns rate-limit, advance clock by computed delay, assert same request payload is resent, second call success is returned from `call()` without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: On max retry exhaustion, client returns typed `QuotaExhaustedError` instead of generic error.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE (quote): "After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error." Adjacent lower-level miss: Unit-testing only the error class or mapper can pass while orchestrator emits transport/generic error due to incorrect terminal-state mapping after retry loop. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; stubbed repeated 429s suffice, so not System; (b) no AI dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because text constrains behavior to retry-count terminal mapping, not real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run `call()` through orchestrator with stub transport that always rate-limits and configured max retries; assert final rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` type and not generic/underlying transport error.

behaviors.description: End-to-end retry chain (initial attempt, retries, shared quota accounting) terminates and never loops indefinitely before surfacing failure.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE (quote): "The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely." Adjacent lower-level miss: Unit tests of loop counter/backoff function can pass while combined state machine (attempt counter + quota state + scheduler) fails to reach terminal condition and spins. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) termination bug can be reproduced in-process with fake clock and stub responses, so System not required; (b) no AI output; (c) no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text is about internal termination/state progression, not real OS/network timing primitives.
behaviors.test_description: With fake timers and deterministic stub responses that keep rate-limiting, execute `call()` and assert bounded number of attempts, bounded scheduled waits, and terminal `QuotaExhaustedError` within expected virtual time.

behaviors.description: Concurrent async callers in one process consume a single shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE (quote): "Multiple callers within the same process share the same account quota" and "Requests issued concurrently ... all draw from the same shared quota bucket." Adjacent lower-level miss: Unit-testing bucket math alone can pass while per-client instances accidentally maintain separate counters under concurrent `call()` paths. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) requires concurrency across local modules but not real network/OS primitives, so not System; (b) no AI dependency; (c) no multi-agent steps. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior explicitly scopes to "same process" shared state, reproducible with in-process async tasks.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate library in-process, launch concurrent `call()` invocations through multiple callers using a stubbed API, and assert aggregate quota consumption/enforcement is global (not per-caller) under overlap.

behaviors.description: Queued waiting requests are dispatched FIFO by original submission order.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE (quote): "When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted." Adjacent lower-level miss: Unit-testing queue helper may pass while real dispatcher integration reorders due to async wake-up/race between scheduler and sender. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) in-process controlled scheduler/fake timers suffice; no real OS/network/subprocess needed, so not System; (b) no AI dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because required failure is ordering across internal async modules, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Drive several `call()` requests into waiting state, release quota/advance virtual time, and assert send order and completion order follow submission FIFO at dispatcher boundary.
